name: Rolled Calzones
recipeIngredient: Chopped fresh rosemary, tarragon, and oregano
1/8 pound Coppacola (spicy Italian, ham), thinly sliced
2 Sweet red peppers, roasted, peeled, and sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Soften the yeast in warm water. Stir in olive oil, then flour and salt. Knead dough on a floured board until smooth and elastic (about 20 minutes). Shape into a round ball, cover with plastic, and refrigerate for 1 hour.
2. Preheat oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit. Shape the pizza dough into a ball and roll out into a large oval shape (about 24 inches long).
3. About 2 inches behind the front edge, spread pesto in a long stripe, stopping just short of the ends.
4. Just behind and overlapping the pesto, place thin slices of ham, cheese, coppacola, peppers, and pepperoncinis. You should still have about 2 inches of dough showing at the back edge.
5. Beat the egg and brush it on the back edge and ends of the dough.
6. Roll up the calzone as firmly as possible to prevent air from getting inside. Tuck the ends in and finish with the back edge on the bottom, not on the side.
7. Place the rolled calzone on a baking sheet pan sprinkled with cornmeal and brush with egg white. Bake in a 400-degree oven for 20 minutes. Cool to room temperature and slice. Thin slices can be served as appetizers.
Note: To roast peppers, preheat the broiler. Slice the peppers lengthwise, core them, and remove seeds and ribs. Lay the pieces skin side up on a flat broiling pan and place the pan about 3-4 inches below the heat. Broil the peppers until skins are charred (black). Transfer to a plastic bag, seal with a twist tie, and let steam for 15 minutes. Remove from the bag and discard chared skins.
